title: Inventorio Fixes
description: A dirty collection of patches for the mod Inventorio targeting mod support
body:
# Inventorio… fixes?

Made specifically for the [Hardrock TerraFirmaCraft 3](https://www.curseforge.com/minecraft/modpacks/terrafirmacraft-hardrock) modpack!

This project exists as a separate mod due to the 1.18.2 version of Inventorio no longer receiving updates, and as such Inventorio no longer accepting pull requests. I also don't really trust myself to write reliable code yet, so making my own mod is lower stakes >.<

Please support Lizard of Oz, the original creator of Inventorio, and RubixDev, the current maintainer! You can find the modrinth page for the mod [here](https://modrinth.com/mod/inventorio).

## List of patches:

### Inventorio:
*   Fix an edge case where the incorrect main hand may be targeted when creating or deleting an item when a tool is active (this should hopefully fix a good chunk of dupe exploits)
*   Fix incorrect mining speed being used when a tool is selected in the hotbar while another tool from a tool slot is active
*   Fix Inventorio slots not syncing on the client correctly when changing dimensions
*   Make items within the active tool popup dynamically update the item being used, instead of keeping a static cache! This, for example, makes it so that when the tool loses durability, the displayed item will also reflect that.
*   Make the temporary tool slot popup a little more responsive
*   Add extra convenience keybinds for explicitly opening a vanilla inventory, explicitly opening an Inventorio inventory, and changing the currently open inventory without having to reach your mouse to the switch in the top-right corner

The rest are "optional" and are only loaded when the matching mod is present:

### TerraFirmaCraft:
*   Fix Inventorio slots not respecting item stack sizes in some cases
*   Make Inventorio slots count for overburdening effects
*   Make the Stack Food keybind work in the Inventorio inventory

### Create Stuff 'N Additions:
*   Fix dropping an item applying a blank NBT tag to your offhand item, preventing it from stacking with other items of the same type (this is technically not caused by Inventorio but I still really needed to fix this :p)

### Tinkers' Construct:
*   Make the Soulbound modifier work for items that are in an Inventorio slot

### Hardcore Revival:
*   Make the "Press E to die" prompt work with the Inventorio inventory and not just the vanilla one

## Usage in modpacks

Go ahead! :D  
Though, as this mod depends on Inventorio, please give a careful read to Inventorio's terms first: [https://github.com/RubixDev/Inventorio#use-in-modpacks-and-with-other-mods](https://github.com/RubixDev/Inventorio#use-in-modpacks-and-with-other-mods)
